name: Trade Caravans Attacked
Description:
An unknown monster has been attacking our trade caravans as they travel through the mountain, and while we have pinpointed its location, it has yet to be stopped. 1000 eros reward. 
-Elena 

Warning! Previous adventurers sent out to stop the monster have returned with what they report as 'thoroughly attacked rear entrances' and refuse to go into further detail about what kind of monster it even is, so caution is advised! 


A small, messily written note is pinned to the bottom of the quest sheet that reads 'Reward increased to 2500 eros for anyone who can permanently stop the attacks. -Elena'

theScene: After searching around for a while, you finally find the cave where the monster attacking the caravans is supposed to live.
You cautiously approach the entrance to the pitch black cave, and freeze as you hear something, or someone, slowly slithering through the darkness towards you.

As the source of the ominous slithering noise slowly moves closer and closer to you, you strain your eyes to stare into the inky black darkness of the cave and manage to see the faint outline of a towering monster moving towards you as a pair of golden eyes seem to stare holes into your paralyzed form.

As soon as the words leave your mouth, the monster lunges out of the darkness before you can blink and swings her hand down a few inches from your foot, giving you a good look of her wickedly sharp looking reptilian claws as she glares at you in a fit of pure rage!
I! AM! A! {i}WURM{/i}! A {i}REAL{/i} DRACONIC SPECIES! NOT SOME DUMB FLYING, HALF EXCUSE FOR A DRAGON WITH NO REAL HANDS!

As soon as the words leave your mouth, the monster lunges out of the darkness before you can blink and swings her hand down a few inches from your foot, giving you a good look of her wickedly sharp looking reptilian claws as she glares at you in a fit of pure rage!
I! AM! A! {i}WURM{/i}! A DRACONIC SPECIES THAT HAS NOTHING TO DO WITH THOSE FUCKING SNAKE {i}SLUTS{/i}! THEIR TAILS ARE {i}PATHETIC{/i} COMPARED TO MINE!

As soon as the words leave your mouth, the monster lunges out of the darkness before you can blink and swings her hand down a few inches from your foot, giving you a good look of her wickedly sharp looking reptilian claws as she glares at you in a fit of pure rage!
I! AM! A! {i}WURM{/i}! A DRACONIC SPECIES! NOT SOME MEAGER AND WORTHLESS REPTILIAN THAT'S ONLY {i}PITIABLE{/i} NEXT TO SOMEONE LIKE ME!

Her massive tail angrily lashes out and shatters a person sized boulder to your right, making you flinch as small rock fragments fly in all directions. The wurm barely seems to notice, making beads of cold sweat run down your back as the source of such overwhelming power continues to keep all her attention directly squared at you.
She continues to glare at you with contempt, seething with rage until she lets out a spiteful laugh.
Oh... I bet I know why you're here. Yeah... You're just like all the others, aren't you!
Her mouth twists into a vicious grin as she grandly spreads out her arms in challenge while her serpentine tail continues to thrash wildly behind her.
Well, come on then {i}'hero'!{/i} What are you waiting for?
You return to the wurm's lair and cautiously approach the pitch black cave.
The wurm deftly slithers out of the dark cave with a snarl, and looks at you with contempt.
So you've decided to return {i}'hero'{/i}. Back for another round, huh? Well come on then!
Her mouth twists into a vicious grin as she grandly spreads out her arms in challenge while her serpentine tail thrashes wildly behind her.
What are you waiting for? Do it!
You return to Jora's lair and approach the entrance to the pitch black cave.
The wurm deftly slithers out of the dark cave with a snarl and gives you a look of contempt.
But upon realizing that it's you, that contempt quickly transforms into joy and a happy smile blossoms on Jora's face while her tail gleefully swishes behind her.
I thought you said you weren't going to attack the caravans anymore.
The wurm gives you a sheepish grin
Oh uhm... y-yeah, I did... B-But I wanted to see you again, so I figured that you would come back if I did, and I was right!
Seeing as you're here, do you want to see my treasure hoard?
Or are you just going to try and fight me for attacking the caravans? Don't expect me to go easy on you...
You return to Jora's lair and approach the entrance to the pitch black cave.
The wurm deftly slithers out of the dark cave with a snarl and gives you a look of contempt.
But upon realizing that it's you, that contempt quickly transforms into joy and a happy smile blossoms on Jora's face while her tail gleefully swishes behind her.
I thought you said you weren't going to attack the caravans anymore.
The wurm gives you a sheepish grin.
Oh uhm... y-yeah, I did... B-But I wanted to see you again, so I figured that you would come back if I did, and I was right!
Seeing as you're here, do you want to come in and visit for a little while? Maybe take another look at my treasure trove?
Or are you just going to try and fight me for attacking the caravans again? Don't expect me to go easy on you...
You approach the wurm with the intent to defeat her, and she gives you a wide grin before she charges at you with alarming speed!
With the wurm defeated, the caravans should be safe to travel through the mountains again.
You triumphantly return to town and head into the adventurers guild to collect your reward.
Elena gives you a skeptical look as you describe the monster in the cave, but she hands you a pouch with 1000 eros anyway.

Good job [ThePlayerName], but... I can't give you the rest of the reward until we confirm that she isn't going to keep attacking the caravans. If I don't hear anything in the next few days, I'll give you the rest of the reward.
...After a few days the quest is back on the quest board. It seems that the wurm is still attacking the caravans...
With Jora defeated, the caravans should be safe to travel through the mountains again.
You triumphantly return to town and head into the adventurers guild to collect your reward.
Elena gives you a skeptical look as you describe the monster in the cave, but she hands you a pouch with 1000 eros anyway.

Good job [ThePlayerName], but... I can't give you the rest of the reward until we confirm that she isn't going to keep attacking the caravans. If I don't hear anything in the next few days, I'll give you the rest of the reward.
...After a few days the quest is back on the quest board. It seems that Jora is still attacking the caravans...
You immediately turn around and run, deciding that you value your life much more than a simple quest! The wurm clearly wasn't expecting this and freezes for a moment, unsure of what's going on.
Wh-Wha-? WAIT! GET BACK HERE! YOU CAN'T JUST SHOW UP LIKE THIS AND THEN JUST {i}RUN{/i}!
With a cry of anger, the wurm starts to chase you down with a terrifying burst of speed.
But thanks to your head start, you manage to keep out of reach of her as she rapidly loses speed and the distance between the two of you grows larger and larger.
As you continue to sprint down the mountain, you hear to wyrm scream out one last thing to you.

You look over your shoulder to see the wyrm turn around and head back towards her cave, and you start to slow down and catch your breath a little before returning to town, making sure to keep a close eye on the path behind you just in case.
The wurm glares at you for a moment and nods.
Fine, I suppose I'll let you leave.
You had already started to leave, but decide not to say anything. She heads back into her cave with a small grin, and you head back to town.
As you make your way back, you consider turning in the quest, but as you check your grimoire, you notice that it doesn't have any record of you actually defeating the monster, so you decide not to.
You quickly apologize about getting her species wrong, and explain that not only could you not see her very well in the darkness, but also that the quest didn't say what kind of monster she was.
Ha, pre-combat banter, huh?! Well! Prepare to...
The wurm trails off as she realizes what you just said.
Her hostile attitude seems to die down and she looks at you skeptically as you nod at her.
Hmph, well I won't accept your apology unless you properly praise my draconicness.
She crosses her arms and glares at you expectantly, impatiently tapping one of her clawed talons against her forearm.
You apologize for attacking her after praising her.
Ha, pre-combat banter, huh?! Well! Prepare to...
The wurm trails off as she realizes what you just said.
Her hostile attitude seems to die down and she looks at you skeptically as you nod at her.
Hmph, well I won't accept your apology unless you properly praise my draconicness again, and {i}better{/i} this time!
She crosses her arms and glares at you expectantly, impatiently tapping one of her clawed talons against her forearm.
You compliment her tail for how long and powerful it is. It swishes back and forth with joy at your words.
You compliment her sharp, gleaming teeth for how shiny, big, and terrifying they are. She grins happily, showing off her toothy maw.
Hehehe, I wouldn't have thought someone would praise that!♪
You compliment her numerous pointed horns and how awe inspiring they are. She nods her head proudly and grins at your words.
You compliment her scything claws for how intimidating and powerful they look. She looks them over with a smile.
Mhmm, they are pretty great. They can tear through stone no problem!♪ Care to see?
You politely decline, preferring to not see the wicked blades in action.
Really? That's a shame. They really {i}are{/i} impressive.♪ Anything else about me catch your eye?
You mention her lack of draconic wings in an attempt to compliment her, but end up trailing off as her eye twitches and her body starts to shake with rage.
A strained and angry grin slowly spreads over her face.
Without waiting for an answer, the wurm roars in anger and charges you!
Hey! You already praised that! Tell me something else great about me! {i}Or else!{/i}
Alright, now it sounds like you're trying to mock me by complimenting the same thing over and over again... I'm going to be generous and let you try to {i}properly{/i} praise me one last time.
The wurm lets out a nasty snarl as she charges you!
Hmhmhm... I am appeased. Alright, I shall forgive your prior insult, human.
...Hmm... I guess you're still here to rescue that princess, aren't you?
The wurm seems taken aback from your sudden outburst, and you secretly hope you didn't just offend her all over again.
W-Well... No, but I figured that's why all those adventurers kept showing up. Everyone must have just figured that the 'big bad draconic wurm' captured a princess and they needed rescuing.
To accentuate her innocence, she makes some rather cute fake roaring motions after bringing up the 'big bad draconic wurm'.
That's not quite right, the reason why everyone, including me, showed up in the first place was to stop you from attacking all the caravans.
She starts to scratch her face idly with a claw while looking about, deep in thought.
The wurm innocently looks at you after a brief silence.
Nope, I haven't done any of that I don't think. The only thing I do around my territory is collect from the tribute carts that come through every once in a while.

I meet them on the road before they come up to my cave so I can spare them the final part of the trip, but they never seem too pleased to see me so soon. They must want to make the entire trip themselves, but I am gracious enough to save them the trouble.
She has her claws on her hips and seems rather proud...
She seems really happy at your comment, giving you a smug look as she idly flexes her taloned hands.
Ehehe,♪ very draconic of me right?♪ Hmhmhm, even though I told you your were done praising me, you're still going.♪
Her grin falters slightly as she thinks for a moment, then gives you a slightly confused look.
You explain to her that the 'tribute carts' {i}are{/i} the caravans.
The wurm angrily bristles and glares at you as you tell her she's wrong, and you instinctively step back as she menacingly slithers towards you.
W-Wait, What?! That is utter nonsense, they're {i}definitely{/i} tribute carts! Why else would they go near MY territory?
W-Well... It's probably my job to attack them anyways!
You glare at her and she seems to deflate a little.
D-Don't look at me like that! ...Fine! I'll give back the stuff I took and stop attacking the caravans! Is that what you want?
You let out a sigh of relief as the wurm finally lowers her arms and assumes a much less threatening posture, but she still towers above you.
W-Well... Okay, I'd give back the stuff if I didn't, uh... Eat most of it...
I'm... I'm sure it's fine as long as you don't attack the caravans anymore.

You stare at the wurm a little bit longer to make sure she gets the point, and she eventually breaks eye contact and stares at the ground, grumbling softly to herself. Satisfied that you've made your point, you turn around to leave, but stop as the wurm lets out a sudden noise of realization.
Wait a second! In my rage before, I forgot to do my proper and terrifying introduction! I! AM...!
The wurm trails off for a moment as you give her a level look, no longer in the mood for a 'terrifying' introduction.
...Umm... I guess it's a little too late for that, huh...?
Just... Jora. My name's Jora. What's yours?
You tell her your name, happy to finally have a normal interaction with the wurm.
Her tail starts to swish back and forth with excitement.
Hey [ThePlayerName], given that you're here, do you want to see my draconic treasure hoard?
She stares at you for a moment, completely dumbfounded.
Huh? What? No. I mean treasure. TREASURE! ...What about that could possibly be considered sexual?
She trails off for a moment, brow furrowed in concentration as she tries to figure out how what she said could conceivably be thought of as innuendo.
Jora claps her claws together with uncharacteristically giddy joy as she looks at you excitedly.
Before you can take even a single step towards the cave, her tail suddenly lunges forward at blinding speeds and tightly coils around you!
She excitedly brings you into the dark cave, and you're happy that her tail is only {i}slightly{/i} crushing you as memories of the shattered boulder from earlier race through your head.
Thankfully, it doesn't take Jora long to reach her destination, and she frees you from her coiled tail as inky darkness presses in from all sides.

You strain your eyes in an attempt to see anything to no avail, and as you look around you manage to see a small pinprick of light from the entrance to the cave, which surprisingly isn't too far away.
Oh... Right, heh, humans and their inferior non-draconic eyes. One second, now where's that thing...
Jora suddenly turns to look for something, which you only know from the sudden impact of her tail in your stomach painfully smashing you into an unseen wall.
EH?! Oh! Uh, s-sorry, I'm not used to having company so, uh...
You gently rub your stomach as you hear Jora rummaging around in the dark, and imagine that you'll probably have a nasty bruise tomorrow.
Suddenly, the entire cave is flooded with pale light from a rather sizable white gemstone, which appears to have a large bite taken out of it.
There! And, uh, I am really sorry about hitting you with my tail...
She grandly gestures to a small stone slab as your eyes adjust to the sudden light, and you take a moment to absorb the insides of the cave.

The interior of the cave is rather cramped, and the only furniture you can see is a massive bed made from assorted scraps of cloth and other fabrics piled together off in the corner, but before you can continue looking around, your attention is drawn towards the slab that Jora is insistently gesturing towards.

The top of the stone slab is completely smooth and has a few assorted knickknacks on it, a small pile of about 20 gold coins from countries other than Lucidia, and numerous assorted gems and minerals, including the shining gemstone that's currently lighting the room.
You can't help but feel a little underwhelmed after all of Jora's buildup of it, culminating in her rather over the top 'BEHOLD'.
She clearly notices your disappointed expression and frowns a little as she defensively moves towards her hoard.
H-Hey, don't look at my treasure trove like that! I mean... I know that half of it {i}is{/i} food after all... But some of them really ARE important to me!
She eagerly points to the glowing gemstone with the bite taken out of it.
Like this! I was digging through the earth when I found this, and when I took a big bite out of it it scared me half to death by blinding me! Going from pitch black to really bright hurts a lot...
Jora winces slightly in memory before she returns her attention back to you.
But it was one of the coolest gems I've ever found, so I decided to keep it here instead of eating it as revenge for blinding me. It really helps to make some of my other treasures glimmer.
She starts looking over her small pile of treasures, trying to decide what to talk about next...

You head into the darkness of Jora's cave, and she quickly lights up the room with one of her treasures, allowing you to see the cave and it's worn interior as Jora stands between her bed and the stone slab covered with her hoard.
You tell Jora you want to see her treasure again, and clasps her hands together excitedly before gesturing you towards them.
Ask about the glowing gemstone with a bite taken out of it.
Ask about the pile of gold coins.
Ask about the strange looking cup.
Ask about the aged wooden dolls.
Ask about the sword with almost just the hilt left.

EventText.NameOfScene: Ask about the glowing gemstone with a bite taken out of it.
theScene: Well, I was digging through the earth when I found this, and when I took a big bite out of it it scared me half to death by blinding me! Going from pitch black to really bright hurts a lot...
Jora winces slightly in memory before she returns her attention back to you.
But it was one of the coolest gems I've ever found, so I decided to keep it here instead of eating it as revenge for blinding me. It really helps to make some of my other treasures glimmer.
I guess it helps you see in here as well, so I'd say it's an important part of the treasury!

EventText.NameOfScene: Ask about the pile of gold coins.
theScene: She picks up a gold coin with a claw.
Oh, these are just snacks, really tasty snacks.
She throws it into her mouth and chews contentedly.
She picks up one of the coins and brings it close to your face.
You explain to her you can't eat that.
She shrugs and throws the second coin into her mouth.
Poor humans and their inferior eating habits.
Jora idly reaches for another coin, but stops herself and lets out a heavy sigh as she stares off into the distance.
...To be honest, these gold coins are actually really hard to find for some reason.
She gives you a somewhat frustrated look.
You can find tons of those dumb purple coins all over the place, but they taste {i}awful{/i}. I don't know what they're made of, but I can tell you for a fact that it's not a mineral.

EventText.NameOfScene: Ask about the strange looking cup.
theScene: She carefully picks up the strange red cup with her claws.
To be honest, I have no idea what this is or what it's even made of.
I found a bunch of them a long time ago from a harpy when it was carrying a tribute box for me and decided to take a few of them home.
You're pretty sure that wasn't a tribute box, but decide to not interrupt her story.
I tried eating some of them, but they tasted terrible and I got really sick...
They work like normal goblets though, except for some reason they do this.

She starts to lightly squeeze it, and the cup seems to miraculously bend with the pressure, but when she stops putting force on the cup, instead of remaining bent it starts to slowly return to its normal shape before your very eyes.
You stare at the cup in amazement as it continues to slowly reform itself, and eventually it pops back into its original form, looking as if it had never been crushed in the first place.
I'm pretty sure they're magical. They aren't able to regenerate from nothing, though, and I've broken a few of them permanently in the past, but it's still really cool.
She puts it back down on the stone slab.
After experimenting with them a little I decided to only keep one though, so I've got to be careful not to break it. If I had kept all of them, they'd be the only thing in my treasure trove.
You can't help but feel a little disappointed that you can't have one of the magical cups yourself, but you don't dwell on it as you redirect your attention back to the rest of Jora's hoard.

EventText.NameOfScene: Ask about the aged wooden dolls.
theScene: With an almost childlike grin, Jora delicately picks up one of the wooden dolls and brings it up close to her face.
Oh, I've had these since I was just a little wurmling!

After giving it a long look, she gently places it back down on the stone slab and eagerly tells you about the 'brave knight', the 'dainty princess', and the 'monstrous dragon' with a happy little smile.
She doesn't let you touch them, but she lets you get up close and see them, and you note that even though they're well worn with the passing of time, they're exceptionally well crafted.
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Spiritual Moment (1_32) 90bpm (L).mp3
Jora's smile starts to waver for a moment and she sighs wistfully.
I'm gonna be really sad when they finally break... I can't even play with them anymore because they're so old...
She straightens after a brief moment and flashes you a toothy grin, but you can tell it's a little forced.
Not that I {i}would{/i} play with them anymore, mind you.
She carefully puts the dolls back on the stone slab with a sad little sigh.
Jora wistfully stares into the eyes of the dragon doll for a long while before she seems to remember that you're still standing next to her.
Uh, h-hey, let's talk about something else in the trove!
Jora jumps at your gentle question and quickly straightens herself.
H-Huh?! Of course I'm okay! I'm a dragon! W-Well, actually I'm a wurm, which is a subspecies of dragon, b-but that doesn't mean t-that... that...

She looks over at the dolls for a brief moment and her tough facade crumbles as she seems to physically become much smaller, defensively wrapping her massive tail around her body as she stares at her clawed hands.
It's just... Looking at them reminds me of my dad, because he bought them for me a long, long time ago.

And... And he died... what, fifty years ago? A hundred? W-Wait, it was closer to t-two hundred now... I-I'm sorry, it's kind of hard to keep track of time when y-you're underground for long periods of t-time...
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Sense Of Loss (2_04) 54bpm.mp3
A-And it all h-happened when m-mom wasn't there, a-and...
She reaches a claw up to scratch her cheek but stops as she notices the tears streaming down her face for the first time.
Sh-Shit, I got something in my eyes, g-give me one s-second...
You walk up to the crying wurm and give her a hug, and she stiffens for a brief moment before she throws her arms around you, nuzzling her face into your shoulder as she lets out a few heavy sobs.

Jora's arms tightly cling to you with almost bone crushing force, but you don't let any of the pain show as she continues to let out muffled cries into your shoulder, knowing that she needs this far more than anything you could ever want right now.
The hug goes on for a few minutes as she continues to gently sob, but eventually she gets her breathing under control and slowly disentangles herself from you as she rubs her eyes with her arm.
Jora stares at the ground for a long moment before she slowly looks up at you with a look of thanks tinged with profound sadness.
...I don't think I've ever really... talked to anyone about that before, so...
Jora trails off as she stares at you before she continues in an almost inaudible voice.
You say nothing as Jora continues to look at you before she suddenly straightens and her usual cocky grin reappears on her face.
But if you tell {i}anyone{/i} that you saw me crying, I'm going to throw you into a ravine!
She holds the smile for a moment before it starts to waver, and she sighs as she looks down at the ground.
Alright, let's talk about something else...
After a few moments of awkward sniffling, Jora takes a second to steady her breathing and gestures back towards her treasure trove, seemingly intent on distracting herself.

EventText.NameOfScene: Ask about the sword with almost just the hilt left.
theScene: I'll be honest, I just found a sword one day and ate most of the blade... But I thought the hilt looked really cool, so I decided to keep it!
I'd put a skull next to it or something, but I haven't found one yet. Hm, maybe a helmet would be easier to find...?
You decide that you've had enough of looking at Jora's treasures and take a moment to get another, better look at the rest of the cave.

The floors seem to be worn down and slightly grooved from Jora moving around, and as you lean over to feel it, you notice that it's almost smooth to the touch, and aside from the treasure hoard in the center of the room and the bed in the corner, the cave is rather empty.
You turn your attention back to Jora to see her eagerly hold up a clawful of colorful gems and rocks in front of your face.
She slowly returns the handful of rocks back to the stone slab in the center of the room before turning back to you, awkwardly looking around as she tries to figure out what to do next.
You decide that you've had enough of looking at Jora's treasures and think on what to do next.
music/Mountain/Purple Planet Music - Chilled - Desert Winds (3_12).mp3
Jora unexpectedly blushes and starts to fidget with her claws while her tail starts to swish from side to side.
O-Oh, I'm not really in the mood for that... A-And we should probably get to know each other a little bit better before all of that...
Her face flushes completely red and you're about to drop the subject when a sudden thought crosses your mind.
...Wait, but what about the fights with previous adventurers trying to stop the attacks?
She looks at you like you've just asked her the dumbest question in the world.
Huh? That's combat, that's completely different than sex.
Real sex is for couples, lovers, and stuff like that.
music/Mountain/Purple Planet Music - Chilled - Desert Winds (3_12).mp3
Eh?! J-Just because we've taken a nap together doesn't mean we can do something like that!
She fidgets bashfully with a deep flush.
It-It's not like we're a couple yet or anything!
music/Mountain/Purple Planet Music - Chilled - Desert Winds (3_12).mp3
Jora gives you a sleepy smile as her tail starts to reach out and lovingly coil around you before she suddenly freezes and pulls it back.
Ah... W-We can't do something like that! Even if...
She fidgets bashfully with a deep flush before looking away from you.
C-Can we talk about something else please? I-I'm awake now!
Try to get to know Jora better.
music/Mountain/Purple Planet Music - Chilled - Desert Winds (3_12).mp3
Jora grins and suddenly grabs you with her claws, forcefully pulling you towards her bed before gently lowering you down onto it.
Before you can do anything, she pulls you into a tight hug and wraps her tail around your legs, immobilizing you as she happily hums.
Jora cuddles against your body, and you can't help but flush as her sizable breasts press against you and gently vibrate as she continues to hum softly.
But despite being unable to move, you find yourself relaxing more and more into Jora's comfortable grip as she slowly starts to fall asleep.
It doesn't take long for you to follow suit as the sound of her sleepy breathing and her surprisingly soft body lull you to sleep...
Some time later you wake up as Jora starts to move, and she releases her grip on you with a stretch as she lets out a cute yawn and gently rubs her eyes with her forearm.
That was... Nice. Uh... so now what do you want to do?
She sleepily gazes at you with a slight flush.
You want to talk? Sure! About what?
Pffft, what kind of dragon needs {i}friends{/i}? I'm fine here by myself.
But don't worry, you can still come visit to bask in my draconic glory or look at my treasures any time you want.
After all, you're the only person to ever truly understand how great I am.
She proudly crosses her arms with a smile, unintentionally showing off her sizable chest.
Jora stands up straighter with a grin, clearly happy you brought them up.
Well [ThePlayerName], dragons are the best monster species of them all, far greater than any other kind of monster you could ever think of. I should know, after all, I {i}am{/i} one.
Dragons have impressive treasure hoards, deadly intellect, capture fair princesses, and fend off brave knights all the time!
We're what most stories are made of!
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Spiritual Moment (1_32) 90bpm (L).mp3
Jora stops for a moment and looks wistfully towards the entrance of the cave.
...Though... I'll be honest [ThePlayerName]... Sometimes I kind of wish I was a {i}normal{/i} dragon instead of a plain old wurm.
Being able to fly would be so {i}cool{/i}...
She looks over at you as her face scrunches up in thought.
One time I tried to get a bunch of harpies to try and lift me up off the ground so I could see what it's like, but any time I started to get close to them they would just start to fly away.
I guess I'm just too awe inspiring for them to handle, huh?
Jora flashes you a toothy grin as she talks about herself, and you don't have the heart to tell her that the harpies were probably scared out of their minds.
Oh, you want to see my hoard again? Sure, let's-
You clarify your question, making sure to emphasize asking her {i}why{/i} she's collected the things she has.
Jora gives you a funny look.
Why? Well, because I'm a dragon of course!
How else will I lure knights to their doom?
Her face becomes unexpectedly serious as she considers your question.
Well, and I like keeping stuff that's important to me.
Though I DO tend to eat most of the things I find because I get hungry pretty often, and most of the fancy stuff tastes {i}way{/i} better than plain old rocks and dirt.
She taps a claw against her chin with a look of hope.
Maybe someday I'll actually get a big enough pile of gold that I can coil around and sleep on, wouldn't that be exciting!?
You tell her that it certainly would be, and she grins as she absentmindedly picks up a gold coin and eats it, and you think it's probably going to be a while before she manages to collect that much.
Huh? I-I said I won't attack them anymore... R-Really! I swear!
Even if they {i}do{/i} have some really cool stuff in them...
You inform Jora it's about time you got going, and she begrudgingly lets you leave after you assure her you will come back again.
You inform Jora it's about time you got going and start to leave.
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Spiritual Moment (1_32) 90bpm (L).mp3
But before you manage to take more than a single step, one of her clawed hands lunges out and tightly grips one of your arms, stopping you in place.
H-Hey! You can't leave yet, we still have to... uhm...
She frantically looks around the cave, desperately searching for some reason to make you stay with her a little longer.

EventText.NameOfScene: Try to get to know Jora better.

theScene: As you bring up her prideful nature, Jora stands up straighter and puffs out her chest, looking rather pleased with herself.
Hehehe, my pride {i}is{/i} rather impressive isn't it?
It's always good to keep in mind how superior I am compared to those other non-draconic species.
She turns to you with a look of distaste as she considers the other 'inferior' species.
Like those dumb lizard women you find everywhere, they're completely obsessed with finding mates, and they're no better than those stuck up elves down in the forest in my opinion.
She then leans forward and flexes an arm in front of you, and while you don't see any muscles bulging under her skin, you've seen just how powerful she is already to know not to underestimate her.

Even the minotaurs on the mountain are nothing compared to me in strength! After a group of them challenged me to a fight a few years ago and I wiped the floor with them, they aren't even interested in fighting me anymore!
She throws up her hands in exasperation, then pauses and looks at her arms as they sit above her head before she lowers them with a thoughtful look on her face.
And that reminds me of those little bitty harpies that flit around all the time. They're complete cowards, but they are kind of cute, and their wings are really pretty...
Jora turns to you with a toothy grin as she places her hands on her hips in a statuesque figure of glory.
All of those species flee from my awe inspiring presence!
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Spiritual Moment (1_32) 90bpm (L).mp3
Her grin falters for a moment.
As well as anyone else I generally run into...
There's a long pause as Jora stares at the wall while her face contorts with a mixture of sadness and frustration.
Jora jumps a little, apparently forgetting you were there as she recollects herself.
Ah! [ThePlayerName], r-right! Uhm, and most of the humans I've met are pretty pathetic too, but I guess you're alright...
Jora gazes at you with a smile.
I'm... uh... Glad you didn't run away and stuck around. I... I know I can be a bit much to deal with at times...
Jora bristles angrily and glares at you while her tail twitches violently behind her.
Wh- I am {i}not{/i} violent or angry in nature! Why would you say that?!
You give her a level stare as you gesture towards her clearly defensive posture, and she wilts slightly and averts her gaze with a heavy sigh.
...Okay, I know I can be a little hot headed about some stuff at times... But I don't really mean to hurt anyone, it just kind of... happens, you know?
She gives you a sheepish grin.
W-Well, that's because I really {i}did{/i} think they were tribute carts...
I-I'm not attacking them now! I'm just... kind of scaring them so... so you'll show up again.
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Spiritual Moment (1_32) 90bpm (L).mp3
...I guess I could stop doing that now if you really want... I mean, you really {i}did{/i} come back, after all...
But really, it's not like anyone got hurt when I uh... raided the caravans...
They just kind of ran away when they saw me barreling through rocks and stuff...
Wurms... Kind of have a terrifying reputation...
She looks off with a wide grin, and you're pretty sure she's recalling how you praised her when you first met...
Jora gives you an indignant look.
I'm not lonely! I have you! When you're around, that is...
And even when you aren't, I'm perfectly fine! I'm a dragon! Dragons don't need anyone!
Jora frowns for a moment as she considers the paradoxical nature of what she just said.
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Spiritual Moment (1_32) 90bpm (L).mp3
B-But I still want you around, s-so don't just leave...
She trails off and awkwardly squirms while she tries to figure out what to say next, but she eventually sighs and looks at you.
Alright... Maybe I'm just a little bit lonely up here...
Jora suddenly perks up as she seems to think of some kind of solution.
But if you stick around, that won't be a problem anymore!
I'm sorry, but there are other things I need to do, so I can't. What about the local monster girls? There are lots of them and they probably live much closer to you than I do.
None of them are worthy of the friendship of a mighty dragon like me! And besides that, most of them... tend to run away from me when they see me, or they... live too high up on the mountain.
I-I get scared when I look down from really high places, and I feel much better when I'm closer to the base of the mountain...
But... I'll think about making some more friends... For your sake.
You tell her that she'll be doing it for her own sake and not just yours, but she still seems nervous despite your words, and you figure you've said enough already.
Jora glares at you with a terrible fury burning in her eyes.
You quickly decide to drop the matter, as Jora clearly isn't willing to talk about her odd dragon fixation yet.
Jora grumbles a little, but calms down pretty quickly.
Jora seems like she's willing to let you pry into her life a little more...
Try to get to know Jora better.
Jora seems fed up with talking about personal topics.
Alright! Enough of that! Let's do something fun, like look at my treasure hoard, or... uh... maybe we could... relax a little or something?
You promise Jora you will come back again to visit, and this seems to appease her as she releases her grip on your arm.
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Spiritual Moment (1_32) 90bpm (L).mp3
F-Fine... But if you take too long I might 'accidentally' attack another caravan...
You return to town, and you're pretty sure that the quest will pop up again in a few days...
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Spiritual Moment (1_32) 90bpm (L).mp3
Jora recoils away from you, releasing her grip on your arm as she defensively curls her tail around herself.
W-What?! Of course not! I just...
Jora shakes a bit in frustration.
I'm a mighty dragon! I can't get lonely! I just decided you're one of my treasures! So you aren't allowed to leave!
Ah... Th-That came out wrong! D-Don't get the wrong idea! I-I should have said possession! Y-You're just a thing here for my amusement, n-not at all like what I just said.
A-Also, I'm allowing you to leave so long as you promise to come back!
You promise to her that you'll come back and head on your way, and you're pretty sure that Jora just wanted that awkward moment to end.
You return to town, but the quest remains posted...
Jora glares at you with a terrible fury burning in her eyes.
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Spiritual Moment (1_32) 90bpm (L).mp3
But her rage quickly sputters and dies as she frowns and crosses her arms in discomfort.
I know that... I mean... Technically I'm a dragon by subspecies... But...
She lets out a deep sigh and stares at the ground for a long moment before looking up at you.
I... I know what you mean. I guess it's just... just an ideal I've held onto for a long time, you know?
Ever since I was little I kind of wanted to be a dragon, kidnapping princesses, destroying towns...
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Sense Of Loss (2_04) 54bpm.mp3
In a way, I kind of got what I wanted, huh? Everyone I meet tries to run away from me or just wants to fight me because of the caravans. And you're no different.
You start to object, but Jora puts up a hand to stop you from talking.

I'm not saying that you're just another person who would try to fight me to complete the quest out to stop me, I'm just saying that if that quest wasn't there, you and all those other people would never even realize I existed.
Being a 'big mean dragon' isn't as fun as it sounds... I haven't even really been trying to be mean to anyone, but everyone treats me like I'm going to kill them or something.
And it's not like I really want to actually hurt anyone! I-I just get a little worked up sometimes and everyone j-just runs away...
Jora does her best to not look at you as she stares at the wall while the words continue to pour out of her.

I really did try to make some friends when I first moved into this cave, but after so many people ran away or attacked me on sight, I just kind of... accepted my place as the 'big scary wurm living in a cave'...
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Spiritual Moment (1_32) 90bpm (L).mp3
Jora finally looks at you with a small laugh as she wipes away a few tears from the corners of her eyes.
I'll be honest, [ThePlayerName], I've really been tempted to just kidnap you and keep you here forever, but... it just wouldn't be the same, would it?
She suddenly flashes a small, sad smile as more tears well up in her eyes.
And... I don't think I could bear it if you looked at me like some of the other monsters I've met do.
Jora looks down at the ground as a silence fills the cave before she sighs and looks up at you with renewed confidence in her teary eyes.
I guess I can try to make some more friends one more time, and I'll stop attacking the caravans for good this time. You have my word on that.
music/Mountain/Purple Planet Music - Chilled - Desert Winds (3_12).mp3
You let her know you can try to help her make a new friend on the mountain, and Jora smiles a little.
Maybe... But I think you've already helped me a lot... Uhm, this might be a little weird for me to say, but can you head out? I think I've got some things I really want to think on.
With Jora's insistence, you head out of the cave after sharing a short goodbye and return to town.

You breathe a sigh of relief as the quest to stop the caravans from being attacked is no longer posted, but you stop as you notice a new quest has appeared, and this one is specifically for you. Elena informs you that the reward has been transferred to this new quest, and you'll have to complete it to get the full 2500 eros.
Soon after, you're also given a letter addressed to you from Jora. It's barely legible, but from what you can read it sounds like Jora has made a new friend and wants you to come meet her.
